page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an x86_64 server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration is 64.0 GB, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance includes 1890 GB of local disk storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific compute and memory tasks. The server achieves strong ratings in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, with top-tier performance in image processing. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 211.12 GB/sec for read-only and 102.72 GB/sec for write operations. Advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, support accelerated workloads, as demonstrated by strong performance in small-model LLM inference text generation. However, performance drops to a weak rating of 2.77 tokens/sec for large-model text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the D32lds_v6 offers a compute-heavy profile with a reduced memory footprint, making it a cost-efficient choice for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM allocations. The inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage provides high-capacity local scratch space without requiring external storage attachments. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or massive dataset caching.

This server is well-suited for compute-optimized workloads, image processing, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference. It is also appropriate for software compilation, text processing, and applications requiring high-speed local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of memory and 1890 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmarks, with top-tier results in image processing and small-model LLM text generation. The hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy tasks, though the low memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. This instance is best suited for static web serving, image processing, software compilation, and workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM inference.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server with high-speed Intel processors, local storage, and a low memory-to-core ratio for CPU-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core ratio
1890 GB of local disk storage with premium storage capabilities
Strong performance in image processing, static web serving, and small-model LLM inference
Optimized for software compilation, text processing, and CPU-bound workloads
